title: Whale Swallows Kayaker in Shocking Encounter
story: A kayaker's peaceful day turned wild when a humpback whale accidentally swallowed him whole! In 2025, off the coast of Chilean Patagonia, onlookers watched in disbelief as the massive creature briefly engulfed both man and boat. Amazingly, the gentle giant quickly realized its mistake. It released the lucky kayaker, who emerged unharmed but with an unforgettable tale to tell. This rare event happened on February 13, reminding us of nature's awesome power and unpredictability.
activity_question: Where did this surprising whale encounter take place?
follow_up_question: Why do you think the whale released the kayaker so quickly?

title: Lego Overboard: The Great Ocean Toy Spill
story: Splash! A massive wave crashed into the cargo ship Tokio Express off England's coast on February 13, 1997. The ship tipped, sending containers tumbling into the sea. But these weren't ordinary boxes - they held millions of Lego pieces! Suddenly, the ocean was filled with tiny plastic treasures. For years after, beachcombers found colorful Lego bits washed up on shores, turning a shipping accident into an unexpected treasure hunt.

title: A Star-Sized Diamond in Space
story: Astronomers made a dazzling discovery in 2004! On February 13, scientists at the Harvard-Smithsonian Center for Astrophysics found the biggest diamond ever known. But this wasn't a gem you could wear. It was actually a star called BPM 37093, a white dwarf that had crystallized into a diamond the size of a planet. This cosmic jewel sparkles in space, showing us how amazing our universe can be.

title: Stormie's Brave Double Transplant
story: Stormie Jones, a courageous 6-year-old from Texas, made medical history in 1984. On February 13, she became the first person ever to receive both a new heart and liver in a single operation. Doctors worked tirelessly to give Stormie a chance at a healthier life. Her groundbreaking surgery paved the way for future advancements in organ transplants, offering hope to many other children facing similar challenges.

title: River Creates Its Own Weather
story: Imagine a river so massive it makes its own weather! In 2019, intense flooding in Queensland, Australia, caused the Flinders River to swell to an incredible size. On February 13, it stretched about 60 kilometers wide, nearly as broad as some small countries. This enormous expanse of water became so vast that it actually formed its own weather system, influencing local temperatures and even creating clouds above it.

title: Gentle Planet Birth: A Space Discovery
story: Planets aren't born from cosmic crashes! In 2020, scientists made a surprising discovery about how worlds come to be. By studying Arrokoth, an object in the distant Kuiper belt, they found that planets form through gentle clumping, not violent collisions. This breakthrough, published on February 13 in the journal Science, changed our understanding of the universe's beginnings.

title: Ancient Beer Factory Unearthed in Egypt
story: Bubbling with excitement, archaeologists uncovered a surprising find in 2021. Hidden beneath the sands of Abydos, Egypt, they discovered the oldest known beer factory in the world! title: Windstorm Wrecks Washington's Floating Bridge
story: Howling winds whipped across Washington State on February 13, 1979, unleashing their fury on the Hood Canal Bridge. title: Tycho Brahe's Starry Vision

This heroic act became the first military action to earn the Medal of Honor, America's highest military decoration.

He fought off a hundred Indians alone
He flew a hot air balloon for reconnaissance

title: Arctic Shipwreck: The SS Chelyuskin's Icy Fate
story: Crunch! Ice packs squeezed the Soviet steamship SS Chelyuskin like a giant's fist. On February 13, 1934, the vessel met its doom in the frigid Arctic Ocean. title: Brussels Buries Its River
story: Imagine a city without its main river! In 1867 on February 13, Brussels began a massive project to cover the Senne River.
